extra.reasoning:
The property requires that in every reachable state, opted_out=true implies sent=false. Transition [t5] breaks this: it is guarded on (opted_out and ticks>=10) and sets sent:=true, directly contradicting the intent.

Reachability of the violating state:
1. Event 'optout' fires [t1]: opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=0.
2. Ten 'tick' events fire [t4] each time, incrementing ticks 0->10 (capped at 10) and keeping sent=false, opted_out=true. After the 11th event, state is opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=10.
3. Now both [t3] (send_attempt if opted_out) and [t5] (send_attempt if opted_out and ticks>=10) are enabled. [t5] exists specifically for this case and sets sent:=true. After the 12th event, state is opted_out=true, sent=true.

This reachable state has opted_out=true AND sent=true, violating globally(opted_out implies not sent). The trace uses 12 events, within the 14-event bound. (Even under any priority interpretation, [t5] is a listed enabled transition that can fire, so the system is unsafe.)
